danger_signals.visual_form: Predatory animal fighter designs (Panther, Crocodile, Cheetah)
danger_signals.meaning: Dangerous opponents identifiable through predator species choice

danger_signals.visual_form: The Pantha character visual presentation as cult member
danger_signals.meaning: Sinister faction threat indicator through character design

danger_signals.visual_form: Character stance and animation during battle sequences
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy state telegraph through behavioral animation

safety_signals.visual_form: Peaceful species (Bunny monk, Llama authority figure)
safety_signals.meaning: Non-threatening or mentor-class characters signaled through gentle species

safety_signals.visual_form: Dali Llama as champion holder and tournament sanctioner
safety_signals.meaning: Established order and legitimate tournament framework as safety structure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: The Pantha character design distinguishes cult affiliation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arena environments, fighter assembly, championship belt display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All characters gathered on Brutal Island for competition; structured combat

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Champion reign (Dali Llama holds Belt of Heaven)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dali Llama as final boss/champion, belt as visual objective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dali Llama tests warrior spirit; maintains order through tournament structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Characters exist with distinct peacetime identities (monk, rock star, politician)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters pursue individual careers and conflicts outside tournament arena

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Secret character access (Karate Croc, Dali Llama require button sequences)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transformation mechanics (Chung Poe bat-dragon hybrid form during battle)

environmental_storytelling.example: Rabbit = monk (martial discipline), Bear = commando (strength/military), Llama = spiritual authority (ethereal champion name), Panther = cult member (predatory secrecy)
Prince Leon as rock star, Foxy Roxy in lycra as politician/activist, Rhei Rat as Thai boxer—occupations reflected in character design and presentation
Prince Leon has undisclosed vendetta with Tai Cheetah and Kendo Coyote; Tai Cheetah mentors Kendo Coyote—interpersonal conflicts visualized through narrative roster arrangement

environmental_storytelling.technique: Island setting as neutral tournament ground
environmental_storytelling.example: Uncharted Brutal Island as every-four-years gathering place communicates world-state of formalized martial hierarchy transcending faction

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Tests warrior spirit; judges deepest martial qualification; stands as final opponent
Mentors Kendo Coyote; holds veteran status; involved in complex vendetta
Motivated by greed; estranged from mentor Tai Cheetah; engaged in vendetta with Prince Leon
Associated with sinister cult; only available in certain versions (absent from 16-bit versions)
Transforms into anthropomorphic bat-dragon hybrid during battle; visual state-change indicates hidden power
Apparently suffers from hyperactivity; behavioral disorder as character identifier
Claims to be strongest animal; ex-Soviet commando background; displays confidence/arrogance

notes: Brutal: Paws of Fury uses anthropomorphic animal species selection as primary world-state and faction communication system. Character archetypes (predators, monks, politicians, military) telegraph danger and alignment without explicit faction banners. The tournament structure itself is the world-state manifestation—a four-year cycle that brings disparate fighters into structured competition under Dali Llama's spiritual authority. Visual communication relies heavily on character roster diversity, professional identities, and species-appropriate martial arts styles. Secret character access (Dali Llama, Karate Croc) and transformation mechanics (Chung Poe) indicate hidden world-states. Limited environmental detail in source material; faction identity is primarily character-centric rather than location-based.
